zh.Towcester, Northamptonshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特
zh.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers於1415年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特。他於1433年與Lady Elizabeth Wollaton結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1465年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: Rogers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rodgers
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Rogers  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers was born in 1415, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England. He married Lady Elizabeth Wollaton in 1433. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1465, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers est né en 1415, à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Wollaton en 1433. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1465, à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers wurde 1415 in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England geboren. Er hat 1433 Lady Elizabeth Wollaton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1465 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers è nato il nel 1415 nel Towcester, Northamptonshire, England. Lui sposato Lady Elizabeth Wollaton nel 1433. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1465, nel Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogersは1415年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターで生まれた。1433年にLady Elizabeth Wollatonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1465年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers(은)는 1415년 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 태어났다. 그는 1433년 Lady Elizabeth Wollaton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1465년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers nasceu em 1415, em Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Wollaton em 1433. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1465, em Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers родился в 1415 г. в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Wollaton в 1433 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1465 г. в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lord Thomas Joseph Fitz Rogers nació en 1415, en Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Wollaton en 1433. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1465, en Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años.
